definition: Post-GFC credit intermediation shifted from banks (backstopped by reserves, deposit insurance, discount window) to private credit vehicles ($1.7T), CLO equity, and semi-liquid fund structures with no lender of last resort. The supervisory architecture was designed for bank-intermediated credit. Balance sheet normalization does not reverse this migration but removes supportive conditions.
evidence: BoE 2022 LDI crisis as preview of NBFI stress
FSB, BIS, and OFR have flagged the supervisory gap
open_questions: Will supervisory coverage expand to cover NBFI before the next stress event?
Can NBFI stress become systemic without direct central bank backstop?
